reforms.description: The Slovak government implemented several reforms in the banking sector, including an amendment to the Banking Act in January 1996 to enhance competitiveness, the establishment of the Agency for Industrial Development and Revitalisation (AIDR) in 1995 to provide consultancy services, and the creation of the Slovak Credit Institute (SUU) to support enterprise financing needs. Additionally, a Deposit Security Fund was established in March 1996 to guarantee deposits and enhance competition.
reforms.component_name: Agency for Industrial Development and Revitalisation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are designed to enhance competition and support the financing needs of enterprises, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in the banking sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly impact the banking sector but do not constitute a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: This agency will provide consultancy services in technological development, foreign trade, marketing and banking to industries in the process of restructuring.

reforms.description: The Slovak government established the National Agency for the Development of Small and Medium Enterprises (NADSME) in February 1993 to promote the growth and importance of SMEs in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of NADSME is aimed at fostering the growth of SMEs, which are crucial for economic development and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The agency has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in support of SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The development of SMEs has become an important policy issue and, namely, led to the creation in February 1993 of the National Agency for the Development of Small and Medium Enterprises (NADSME).

reforms.description: An Export Promotion Programme was adopted to enhance export capabilities, which includes measures such as simplifying tax returns for exporters and creating an export and import bank to provide credits to domestic enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to improve the export capabilities of domestic enterprises, which can lead to increased economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been adopted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the export capabilities of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: In 1995, an Export Promotion Programme was adopted. It consists of several measures designed with a horizontal approach.
